data.title: France's ENOGIA FY 2025 revenue rises
data.description:
Overview

  • France-based micro-turbomachinery firm's FY 2025 revenue rose 57% to €12.6 mln

  • Order book at end-2025 was €26.8 mln, up 52% over six months

  • Revenue from Innovative Turbomachinery more than doubled to €2.3 mln

Outlook

  • Company anticipates average annual revenue growth of 30% under Turbo 2028 plan

Result Drivers

  • ORC MODULES GROWTH - Revenue from ORC Modules increased by 48%, driven by projects in Singapore, Asia, South America, and Europe

  • TURBOMACHINERY REVENUE DOUBLES - Revenue from Innovative Turbomachinery more than doubled to €2.3 mln, aided by supercritical CO2 projects and engineering contracts

  • ORDER BOOK INCREASE - Order book at end-2025 was €26.8 mln, up 52% over six months, driven by new customer acquisitions and contract extensions

Key Details

Metric

FY Revenue

Beat/Miss

Beat

Actual

EUR 12.57 mln

Consensus Estimate

EUR 12 mln (1 Analyst)

Analyst Coverage

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Enogia SA is €5.50, about 5.5% below its February 11 closing price of €5.82

  • The stock recently traded at 20 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 13 three months ago

Press Release:

For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact reuters.support@thomsonreuters.com.

data.title: Comcast Scores $800 Million Advertising Haul From Super Bowl
data.description:
Comcast walked away from this year's Super Bowl with a massive win about $800 million in advertising revenue.

The company broadcast Super Bowl LX across NBC, Peacock, and NBC Sports Digital, and advertisers showed up in full force. Even though total viewership dipped slightly to 125 million from 127.7 million last year, it was still the second most watched Super Bowl ever. At one point during the second quarter, nearly 138 million people were tuned in.

Comcast said it completely sold out its ad inventory. A standard 30 second commercial cost around $8 million, and about 10 premium slots fetched more than $10 million each. More than 64 brands bought airtime, including heavy hitters like Anheuser Busch .

data.title: AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL GROUP, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
American International Group, Inc. (AIG), a leading global insurance organization, has released its annual Form 10-K report, providing a detailed overview of its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report highlights AIG's commitment to maintaining a strong financial position, enhancing shareholder value, and navigating the complex regulatory environment.

Financial Highlights

While the specific financial metrics such as Revenue, Gross Profit, Operating Income, Net Income, and Net Income Per Share were not provided in the summary, these key metrics are typically detailed in Part 2 Item 8 and Part 2 Item 7 of the Form 10-K report. These sections provide a comprehensive view of AIG's financial performance, including commentary on financial performance, efficiency, cost reduction, and cash accumulation.

Business Highlights

  • Operating Structure: AIG operates through three main segments: North America Commercial, International Commercial, and Global Personal. Additionally, Other Operations include net investment income from the AIG Parent liquidity portfolio and Corebridge Financial, Inc. dividend income.
  • General Insurance Business: The General Insurance segment includes major operating companies such as National Union Fire Insurance Company, American Home Assurance Company, and Lexington Insurance Company. This segment offers a wide range of commercial lines products including property, casualty, financial lines, and global specialty products.
  • Commercial Lines Products: AIG's commercial lines include Property & Short Tail, Casualty, Financial Lines, and Global Specialty products, covering a variety of risks from natural disasters to professional liability and political risk.
  • Personal Insurance Products: The Global Accident & Health and Personal Lines products offer coverage for personal auto, homeowners, and high net-worth individuals.
  • Human Capital Management: AIG emphasizes retaining, developing, and attracting high-caliber talent, with approximately 22,100 employees across 45 countries. Key initiatives include competitive compensation, health and wellness programs, and talent development opportunities.
  • Regulatory Environment: AIG's insurance subsidiaries are subject to extensive regulation across various jurisdictions, including the U.S., EU, UK, and Bermuda. The company is impacted by laws related to privacy, cybersecurity, and financial services.
  • Privacy and Cybersecurity: AIG complies with various privacy and data protection laws, including the GDPR in the EU and the CCPA in California. The company has implemented measures to protect personal data and manage cybersecurity risks.
  • Sustainability Focus: AIG is actively monitoring regulatory developments related to climate change and sustainability, which are increasingly scrutinized by lawmakers and regulators in the U.S. and other major markets.
  • Future Outlook: AIG anticipates continued evolution in U.S. and international laws and regulations affecting its operations. The company is preparing for new requirements related to privacy, cybersecurity, and sustainability.

Strategic Initiatives

AIG has undertaken several strategic initiatives to strengthen its financial position and enhance shareholder value. These include amending and restating its Certificate of Incorporation and By-laws to reflect updated governance practices. Additionally, the company has entered into various supplemental indentures to manage its long-term debt obligations effectively, ensuring favorable terms and conditions for its securities.

Capital management activities have been robust, with the company executing multiple supplemental indentures to manage its debt portfolio, including the issuance of notes due in 2028, 2029, 2030, 2033, 2034, and 2035. The company has also engaged in stock incentive plans and award agreements to align management and employee interests with those of shareholders. Furthermore, the company has maintained a focus on liquidity and capital resources, as evidenced by its credit agreements and reinsurance arrangements.

Looking ahead, AIG plans to continue optimizing its capital structure through strategic debt management and equity incentive programs. The focus will remain on maintaining a strong balance sheet while exploring opportunities for growth and investment in key areas. The company is committed to enhancing shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation and strategic initiatives that support long-term sustainability.

Challenges and Risks

AIG faces significant market risks due to potential deterioration of economic conditions, geopolitical tensions, and changes in global capital markets, which could materially affect its business, financial condition, and liquidity. The unpredictability of insurance liability claims and the potential inadequacy of reinsurance coverage pose operational risks. Climate change is an emerging risk, contributing to increased frequency and severity of natural disasters, impacting underwriting, claims, and investments.

Investment portfolio concentration in certain economic segments exposes the company to market risks, with potential adverse effects from changes in interest rates, credit spreads, and equity prices. The reliance on third-party investment managers introduces additional risks, as their performance may not guarantee future returns.

Liquidity and capital risks are highlighted by the limited ability of AIG Parent to access funds from subsidiaries, potentially affecting operations and financial stability. A downgrade in credit ratings could further limit access to capital markets and increase borrowing costs.

Operational risks include the potential ineffectiveness of risk management policies, challenges in maintaining critical technology systems, and safeguarding data integrity. The development and use of new technologies, such as generative AI, present additional risks related to data misuse, model flaws, and regulatory scrutiny.

Regulatory risks are significant, with the company subject to extensive and potentially conflicting laws across jurisdictions. Changes in laws and regulations, particularly those related to AI and data analytics, could impact business operations and profitability.

Management acknowledges the challenges posed by market volatility and economic uncertainty. Strategies to address these include enhancing risk management frameworks, diversifying investment portfolios, and strengthening liquidity positions. The company is also focused on improving operational efficiency through restructuring initiatives and technological advancements.

The company is exposed to interest rate fluctuations, which could affect the value of its investment portfolio and increase financing costs. Foreign currency exchange rate volatility also poses a risk, potentially impacting financial results. Management is actively monitoring these risks and implementing hedging strategies to mitigate potential adverse effects.

SEC Filing: AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL GROUP, INC. [ AIG ] - 10-K - Feb. 12, 2026

data.title: SJOVA: 2025 net profit was ISK 2,066 million, with a 92.6% combined ratio and lower investment returns
data.description:
Net profit after tax for 2025 was ISK 2,066 million, with a combined ratio of 92.6%. Insurance operations were strong, but investment income declined sharply year-over-year. 2026 guidance expects profit from insurance contracts of ISK 1,800–2,500 million and a combined ratio of 93–95%.

data.title: Shippers Stick to Sustainability Goals, Survey Shows — Market Talk
data.description: Shippers are pressing ahead with sustainability efforts despite easing regulations, transport technology firm Breakthrough says. In a survey of 500 transport decision-makers, 69% of shippers said they expect to lower their transport-related emissions this year. "In continuously refining their networks to reduce emissions, they're also lowering fuel consumption and limiting exposure to fuel volatility and broader market uncertainty," Breakthrough says. Among measures taken are route optimizing and investment in fuel-efficient vehicles. A soft freight market, meanwhile, led most carriers to reduce, delay or cancel planned equipment upgrades, hiring or other investments in 2025. Almost half expect to delay investments this year, while 36% said they expect to accelerate investments. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Yanik Guillemette of Accolad Introduces an Intelligent Employee Recognition Assistant
data.description:
Montreal, Quebec--(Newsfile Corp. - February 12, 2026) - As artificial intelligence evolves from reactive tools to proactive decision-support systems, Accolad announces the launch of a next-generation AI assistant designed to help managers elevate employee recognition. Inspired by the major technology trends unveiled at CES 2026, this innovation aims to strengthen engagement, productivity, and the human dimension of work.

Yanik Guillemette Québec Entrepreneur

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/11862/283713_64a3f58324d74a67_001full.jpg

From Reactive AI to Proactive Intelligence: A Strategic Shift

The announcements at the 2026 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) confirmed a fundamental transition in AI adoption: artificial intelligence is no longer limited to responding to prompts. It now acts as a proactive agent capable of anticipating needs, recommending actions, and supporting decision-making in real time.

"We are entering an era where AI becomes embedded productivity infrastructure inside organizations," said Yanik Guillemette, Chairman of Accolad's External AI Advisory Committee. "The real question is no longer whether companies should use AI, but how they can leverage it to empower people without replacing them."

An AI Assistant at the Core of Employee Recognition Programs

Aligned with this vision, Accolad has integrated a proactive AI assistant directly into its employee recognition platform. The system continuously analyzes key organizational signals - work anniversaries, years of service milestones, project achievements, and performance moments - and generates personalized recognition recommendations.

The objective is clear: transform employee recognition from a sporadic initiative into a structured leadership reflex.

Rather than being a purely technical feature, Accolad's AI assistant functions as an organizational accelerator:

  • It automates reminders and drafts recognition messages for managers
  • It supports the deployment of fair, consistent workplace recognition programs
  • It frees managerial time for human coaching and talent development

Human-Centered AI with Strong Data Governance

Accolad emphasizes a core principle: technology should amplify human intent, not override it. The AI assistant operates as a co-pilot, while final recognition decisions always remain in the hands of managers.

The platform adheres to strict data governance and privacy standards aligned with Canadian and international best practices. Recommendations are explainable, auditable, and calibrated according to employee preferences - reinforcing trust in the employee recognition program.

Responsible Innovation Guided by an External Advisory Committee

To ensure ethical and sustainable AI deployment, Accolad has established an independent External AI Advisory Committee composed of experts in technology, human resources, and social sciences. The committee's mandate includes reviewing algorithmic impact, decision transparency, and evolving usage patterns.

"AI should help organizations anticipate human needs - not standardize them," added Yanik Guillemette. "A high-performing employee recognition platform strengthens culture, celebrates years of service, and reinforces a sense of belonging."

A Strategic Lever for Canadian Competitiveness

Recent economic analyses indicate that AI adoption in the United States continues to accelerate, while Canada still faces an opportunity gap despite strong innovation capacity. Various studies suggest that between 60% and 80% of U.S. companies have integrated AI into their operations, compared to lower overall penetration rates in Canada.

Organizations that embed intelligent systems within their employee recognition programs are already reporting measurable gains in engagement, retention, and leadership effectiveness.

With its proactive AI assistant, Accolad delivers a practical and locally adapted solution - a recognition platform designed to enhance organizational performance while preserving the human core of work.

About Yanik Guillemette

Yanik Guillemette is a Canadian technology entrepreneur focused on responsible innovation and AI-driven productivity. He is involved in ventures such as FranShares, Bezel, and Guru Organic Energy. As Chairman of Accolad's External AI Advisory Committee, he works at the intersection of artificial intelligence, workforce engagement, and organizational performance. He frequently speaks on AI adoption, workplace recognition, and Canadian competitiveness.

About Accolad

Accolad is a Canadian company that develops an employee recognition platform dedicated to engagement, service milestone recognition, and talent appreciation. By combining artificial intelligence, data science, and HR expertise, Accolad enables organizations to build sustainable, measurable, and human-centered workplace recognition programs.

Media Contact:

Julie Tessier

Head of Communications

Accolad

Email: j.tessier@accolad.com

Phone: 579-274-1477

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/283713

data.title: Diös initiates share buyback program
data.description:
The Board of Directors of Diös Fastigheter AB (publ) has today resolved to utilize the authorization granted by the Annual General Meeting 2025 to initiate a share buyback program. The buybacks will be carried out on Nasdaq Stockholm and will continue until the Annual General Meeting 2026.

The purpose of the buyback program is to optimize the company’s capital structure in order to create increased shareholder value. The decision is based on the company’s strong and stable cash flows and the assessment that the Diös share is trading at a relatively significant discount to its current net asset value.

The buyback program is not expected to impose any limitations on, or have any negative impact on, the company’s financial position. The dividend policy remains unchanged, and the company’s long-term growth strategy continues to focus on investments in the existing portfolio, new developments and acquisitions. Accordingly, the share buybacks will not be carried out at the expense of property investments or value creation in the core business.

Repurchased shares may be used as consideration in potential future acquisitions.

The Board’s resolution covers share buybacks of up to a maximum amount of SEK 200 million. Buybacks may only be executed on Nasdaq Stockholm within the prevailing price interval between the highest bid price and the lowest ask price, and otherwise in accordance with Nasdaq Stockholm’s Rulebook for Issuers and the EU Market Abuse Regulation (MAR). Reporting of completed buybacks will be made in accordance with applicable regulations.

The total number of shares in Diös amounts to 141,785,165, and at the time of this press release the company holds no treasury shares.

ABG Sundal Collier has acted as financial adviser.

For further information, please contact:

  • David Carlsson, CEO, DiösPhone: +46 10-470 95 01E-mail:david.carlsson@dios.se
  • Johan Dernmar, Chief Investor Relations OfficerTelefon: +46 10-470 95 20E-mail:johan.dernmar@dios.se

This information is information that Diös is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the contact persons set out above, at 2026-02-12 17:50 CET.

Attachments

Press Release Diös initiate buy back programme

data.title: McDonald's Posts 6.8% US Sales Growth, Fastest Since 2023
data.description:
McDonald's just delivered something the market has been waiting for: tangible proof that its value pivot could be working. In the fourth quarter, U.S. comparable sales rose 6.8% year over year the fastest pace in more than two years and the strongest showing since 2023 rebounding from a period when traffic had been pressured by an E. coli outbreak. The result came in ahead of analyst estimates, and adjusted earnings also topped consensus. Comparable sales across both international divisions exceeded expectations as well, suggesting the momentum was broader than just the domestic business.

Management has made affordability the centerpiece of its strategy after pandemic-era price increases risked dulling its value perception. With value meals priced as low as $5 and a renewed emphasis on sharper entry price points, the company appears to be regaining traction with cost-conscious diners. Chief Executive Officer Chris Kempczinski stated on the conference call that McDonald's is not going to get beat on value and affordability, adding that the chain gained market share with low-income consumers in December. Executives also cited improved U.S. traffic and higher average spend per visit, supported by promotions such as Monopoly and a Grinch-themed meal, the latter helping drive what the company described as its best sales day ever.

That said, the near-term setup may moderate. Chief Financial Officer Ian Borden indicated that first-quarter comparable sales are likely to slow from the fourth quarter, reflecting the outsized lift from prior promotions as well as severe weather that reduced restaurant hours and kept some diners away. The company recorded an $80 million pre-tax restructuring charge, equal to 9 cents per share, tied to efforts to modernize ways of working under its latest strategic plan, and operating margin declined sequentially. Looking ahead, McDonald's plans to introduce new beverages under the McCafe brand this year including indulgent coffees, refreshers, energy beverages and crafted sodas with ambitions to expand across its global system following a small test in Australia at the end of 2025, potentially adding another lever for traffic and ticket growth if execution holds.

data.title: Bitdeer swings to Q4 profit as revenue triples, shares fall 8% after earnings
data.description:
Bitdeer Technologies Group swung to a profit in the fourth quarter as revenue surged on the back of a major expansion in its bitcoin self-mining operations.

The Singapore-based miner and infrastructure firm posted nearly $225 million in revenue for the quarter ending Dec. 31, up from $69 million a year earlier. Net profit reached $70.5 million, compared with a $531.9 million loss in the same period in 2024, according to results released Thursday.

Growth was driven primarily by Bitdeer’s self-mining business, which generated $168.6 million in revenue during the quarter, up from $41.5 million last year. The company mined 1,673 bitcoins in the fourth quarter, compared with 469 a year earlier, as its deployed hashrate scaled rapidly through 2025.

Total hash rate under management climbed to 71 exahashes per second, more than triple last year’s level, making Bitdeer one of the largest publicly traded bitcoin miners by computing capacity. The figure includes 55.2 EH/s of self-mining capacity, alongside rigs hosted for customers, allowing Bitdeer to surpass rival MARA Holdings on the managed hashrate metric.

The company held just over 2,000 bitcoin on its balance sheet at quarter's end. However, BitcoinTreasuries data shows Bitdeer has since reduced those holdings to roughly 1,040 BTC after selling portions of its treasury in the first two months of 2026, likely to help fund continued mining and AI infrastructure expansion.

The Block reached out to Bitdeer for comment but did not receive an immediate response.

Pressures and AI infrastructure expansion

Despite this, profitability pressures remain. Gross margin slipped to 4.7% from 7.4% a year earlier as electricity expenses and depreciation costs climbed alongside the rapid deployment of new mining rigs. Operating expenses also rose, with research and development spending increasing as the company continued investing in its proprietary SEALMINER chip lineup and AI-focused infrastructure initiatives.

Management said the company is increasingly positioning itself as both a bitcoin miner and a provider of data center capacity for artificial intelligence and high-performance computing customers.

Bitdeer currently reports roughly 3 gigawatts of global power capacity across operating and pipeline projects, with several sites slated for partial conversion to AI workloads over the next two years.

Shares of Bitdeer (BTDR) fell more than 8% on Thursday, dipping below $11 following the earnings release, marking a new year-to-date low according to The Block price data.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

© 2026 The Block. All Rights Reserved. This article is provided for informational purposes only. It is not offered or intended to be used as legal, tax, investment, financial, or other advice.

data.title: MOVING iMAGE TECHNOLOGIES INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Moving iMAGE Technologies Inc., a leading provider of technology solutions for the cinema industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the three months ended December 31, 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in financial performance, driven by higher one-time sales and enhanced gross margins. The company also outlines its strategic initiatives aimed at sustaining growth and expanding its market presence.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $3.793 million, increased by 10.2% from $3.441 million in the prior year due to higher one-time sales.
  • Gross Profit: $1.165 million, increased by 24.5% from $0.936 million, with gross profit percentage rising to 30.7% from 27.2% due to higher margin product revenues.
  • Operating (Loss): $(0.408) million, improved from $(0.561) million, reflecting higher gross margins offset by increased selling, general, and administrative expenses.
  • Net Income (Loss): $(0.388) million, improved from $(0.527) million, driven by higher gross margins and cost control measures.
  • Basic Earnings (Loss) per Share: $(0.04), improved from $(0.05) in the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported a 10.2% increase in net sales for the three months ended December 31, 2025, driven by higher one-time sales. Recurring sales revenues are estimated at $2 million quarterly.
  • Gross Profit Improvement: Gross profit increased by 24.5% to $1.165 million for the three months ended December 31, 2025, with gross profit percentage rising to 30.7% from 27.2% due to higher margin product revenues.
  • Research and Development: R&D expenses remained consistent at $47,000 for both the three months ended December 31, 2025, and December 31, 2024, indicating stable investment in product development.
  • Selling, General and Administrative Expenses: These expenses increased by 5.2% due to higher legal expenses related to the October 2025 DCS inventory purchase.
  • Operational Strategy: The company plans to invest in sales and support operations to support new product initiatives and budget goals, aiming to expand its customer base and increase sales to existing customers.
  • Brand Promotion and Product Offering: The company intends to increase marketing expenditures to enhance brand recognition and introduce new products and services, including new versions of existing product lines.
  • Gross Margin Strategy: The company aims to maintain gross margins by focusing on increased sales volume, reducing operating costs, and introducing new products with higher margins, such as a new system compliant with the Americans with Disabilities Act and Direct View LED screens.
  • Trade Disputes Impact: Potential trade disputes and tariffs could affect the company's operations and financial results, with uncertainty around future trade policies impacting business strategy.
  • Fluctuations in Revenues and Earnings: The company acknowledges that sales and contract fulfillment cycles depend on customer requirements, which can lead to fluctuations in revenue and earnings.
  • Future Outlook: The company believes it has sufficient cash to sustain operations and plans to continue evaluating opportunities to expand operations while controlling costs to meet revenue objectives.

SEC Filing: MOVING iMAGE TECHNOLOGIES INC. [ MITQ ] - 10-Q - Feb. 12, 2026

data.title: ARVN Stock Drops On CEO Exit — Retail Is Bullish On New Chief’s Credentials, Dismisses Selloff
data.description:
Key points:
  • Arvinas’ newly elected Chair of the Board of Director said that the company chose Randy as CEO “after a comprehensive search.”
  • Arvinas is pioneering the development of protein degradation therapies with the help of its PROTAC protein degrader platform.

Shares of Arvinas, Inc. (ARVN) slumped 11% on Thursday after the company announced that it has appointed Randy Teel as CEO, succeeding John Houston.

Teel has been appointed as President, CEO, as well as a member of the company’s Board of Directors, effective immediately, the clinical-stage biotechnology company said.

Teel was previously the firm’s Chief Business Officer and played a key role in the company’s 2018 initial public offering and subsequent transition to a public company. He also served in other key roles including as Arvinas’ interim Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer.

Arvinas’ newly elected Chair of the Board of Director said that the company chose Randy as CEO “after a comprehensive search.” Before Arvinas, Teel worked with Alexion Pharmaceuticals and as an Associate Partner at McKinsey & Company.

“Following our first-ever successful pivotal trial of a PROTAC degrader, we’re directing our attention to our earlier stage clinical programs and their potential to transform treatment paradigms for patients with serious diseases,” Teel said.

Houston, meanwhile, is retiring as President, Chief Executive Officer, and Chair of Arvinas’ Board of Directors. He will continue as a member on the Board and will provide consulting advisory services to the Connecticut-based company, Arvinas said.

What Does Arvinas Do?

Arvinas is pioneering the development of protein degradation therapies with the help of its PROTAC protein degrader platform. These therapies are designed to harness the body’s natural protein disposal system to selectively and efficiently degrade and remove disease-causing proteins.

Arvinas is currently progressing multiple investigational drugs for neurodegenerative disorders, for relapsed/refractory non-Hodgkin Lymphoma, mutated cancers including pancreatic and colorectal cancers, and certain breast cancers.

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around ARVN stock rose from ‘neutral’ to ‘bullish’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume remained at ‘high’ levels.

A Stocktwits user termed the stock drop an overreaction. “This is a planned passing of the torch, not a ‘jump ship’ scenario,” they wrote, while also highlighting the new CEO’s expertise in strategy.

https://www.stocktwits.com/BioSpecialOne/message/644784311

Another user termed the stock a “goldmine” and dismissed the selloff as a result of panic. “...new CEO has the credentials to only help excel PROTAC in the market, dips for more to jump in!,” they said.

https://www.stocktwits.com/NewGuyHere94/message/644790975

ARVN stock has dropped 35% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Ubisoft Allocates Game-Developing Studios to New Units as Part of Structural Changes
data.description:
(16:45 GMT) *Ubisoft Allocates Studios to Creative Houses
(16:45 GMT) *Ubisoft to Appoint Leadership for Creative Houses Starting in March
(16:45 GMT) *Ubisoft 3Q Net Bookings EUR338M
(16:45 GMT) *Ubisoft Backs FY26
(16:45 GMT) *Ubisoft Sees FY26 Net Bookings of Around EUR1.5B
(16:45 GMT) *Ubisoft Sees FY26 non-IFRS Oper Loss of Around EUR1B
(16:45 GMT) *Ubisoft: Consolidated Cash & Cash Equivalents at End of March Expected at Between EUR1.25B and EUR1.35B to Service Debt Maturities
(16:45 GMT) Ubisoft Allocates Game-Developing Studios to New Units as Part of Structural Changes

By Mauro Orru

Ubisoft Entertainment allocated the studios behind its most popular games to new business units as the "Assassin's Creed" maker overhauls its operations and gaming portfolio.

The French videogame group said Vantage Studios, the first of five so-called creative houses it is establishing to focus on specific sets of games, would include development teams based in Montreal, Sofia and Barcelona, among others.

Vantage Studios is responsible for games like "Assassin's Creed," "Far Cry" and "Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six" franchises. Backed by Chinese internet company Tencent Holdings, the unit has been operational since October.

Ubisoft last month announced four other creative houses as part of a new structure that will be operational in early April. The second creative house will focus on shooter games and will include brands like "The Division," "Ghost Recon," and "Splinter Cell."

A third house dedicated to live experiences will include "For Honor," "The Crew," "Riders Republic," "Brawlhalla," and "Skull & Bones." The two remaining houses will focus on immersive fantasy worlds and narrative-driven games like "Rayman" and "Prince of Persia" as well as casual and family-friendly games like "Just Dance."

The company assigned studios to all five creative houses and said it would appoint leadership teams starting next month. For now, Vantage Studios is the only creative house with a leadership team of two co-chief executives in place.

Ubisoft is hoping the new structure will help it reduce complexity and streamline decision-making after it discontinued and postponed several games, closed studios and cut financial targets.

Ubisoft was riding high when lockdowns and other pandemic-era restrictions boosted player engagement as people spent more time at home. However, the lifting of those restrictions and an increasingly competitive market forced it to issue several profit warnings and focus resources on games that were more likely to prove big hits with players. Its stock is down about 90% since the end of 2021 and 35% over the past 12 months.

The group is discontinuing six games, including a remake of "Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time," four unannounced titles and a mobile game. The company closed its Halifax mobile studio in Canada as well as the Stockholm studio and is restructuring its Abu Dhabi, Helsinki and Malmo teams.

Ubisoft said net bookings for the quarter ended Dec. 31 came in at 338 million euros ($401.3 million), up 12% on year and in line with preliminary figures it disclosed last month.

The company said it continued to expect net bookings of around 1.5 billion euros and a non-IFRS operating loss of around 1 billion euros for the year to the end of March. It also expects between 1.25 billion euros and 1.35 billion euros in cash available to service debt maturities.

Write to Mauro Orru at mauro.orru@wsj.com
